title: Statewide transit priority project list
analysis_summary: Create a regularly maintainable statewide list that connects transit-priority projects to location needs, applicable plans, and eligible funding.
analysis_themes: Planning and governance | Funding and grants
qualifiers: where applicable [applies to: consistency with relevant local or regional transportation plans]

title: State mobility-management role
analysis_summary: Establish a statewide mobility-management role that coordinates trips and shared planning, scheduling, payment, and asset technologies across grantees.
mentioned_organizations: Federal Transit Administration | Transit grantees and subrecipients
analysis_themes: Planning and governance | Data, payments, and shared systems
qualifiers: as defined by the Federal Transit Administration [applies to: Caltrans Mobility Manager role]

title: Section 5307 direct-recipient option
analysis_summary: Offer eligible Section 5307 jurisdictions the option to use Caltrans as direct recipient and coordinate resulting federal changes.
analysis_themes: Funding and grants | Planning and governance
qualifiers: if they elect to do so [applies to: jurisdictions choosing Caltrans as direct recipient]

title: Service-led State Transit Plan
analysis_summary: Prepare future State Transit Plan iterations to use service-led planning aligned with the State Rail Plan.
analysis_themes: Planning and governance
qualifiers: future iterations [applies to: State Transit Plan adoption of a service-led planning approach]

title: Transit infrastructure design and permitting materials
analysis_summary: Start and complete a coordinated set of design, specification, policy, and permitting materials for transit infrastructure on the State Highway System.
analysis_themes: Project delivery and permitting | Transit priority and complete trips
qualifiers: Begin the process [applies to: development of design guidance, standard plans and specifications, policy, and permitting procedures]
timing_source_text: Within 120 days of this Order | within one year

title: CEQA transit-delivery report
analysis_summary: Explain to the transportation agency how recent CEQA changes can be applied to expedite transit projects after consultation with local partners.
analysis_themes: Environmental review and public assets | Reporting and administration
qualifiers: including but not limited to, Senate Bill 71 (Chapter 742, Statutes of 2025) [applies to: recent changes to CEQA covered by the report] | in consultation with local partners [applies to: use of recent CEQA changes to expedite transit projects]

title: Active transportation delivery support team
analysis_summary: Create a statewide team that can add technical and delivery capacity for state-funded active-transportation projects.
analysis_themes: Project delivery and permitting | Transit priority and complete trips
qualifiers: projects funded with state resources [applies to: Active Transportation Program projects supported by the team] | where feasible [applies to: partnering with local and regional agencies to provide additional project delivery capacity]

title: Transit and rail funding dashboards
analysis_summary: Create funding-program dashboards covering applications, awards, contracting, reimbursement, and other performance measures to support transparency and equitable access.
analysis_themes: Funding and grants | Data, payments, and shared systems
qualifiers: including but not limited to [applies to: the listed dashboard measures] | as grant funding is consolidated into a system of record [applies to: addition of relevant performance measures]

title: Federal formula-fund flexibility
analysis_summary: Explore lawful ways to help regions shift federal highway formula funds to transit, including possible state-share matching.
mentioned_organizations: Federal Highway Administration | Regions reprogramming federal formula funds
analysis_themes: Funding and grants
qualifiers: as allowable by law [applies to: reprogramming FHWA formula funds for transit uses] | where available and appropriate [applies to: matching state share dollars]

title: Small-agency TDA and NTD reporting automation
analysis_summary: Take steps to automate Transportation Development Act and National Transit Database reporting for rural and small agencies.
analysis_themes: Reporting and administration | Data, payments, and shared systems
qualifiers: Take steps to automate [applies to: Transportation Development Act and National Transit Database reporting] | for rural and small agencies [applies to: agencies covered by the reporting-automation action]

title: Faster and more flexible fund distribution
analysis_summary: Reduce fund-distribution timelines where feasible and examine additional flexibility for uses and federal pre-award authority.
analysis_themes: Funding and grants
qualifiers: wherever feasible [applies to: reducing timelines for distribution of funds] | where practicable [applies to: lifting limits imposed on use of funds] | where allowed [applies to: providing pre-award authority for federal funds]

title: Federal environmental-review assignment
analysis_summary: Begin pursuing federal environmental-review assignment for transit and commuter-rail projects, subject to federal law.
mentioned_organizations: United States Department of Transportation | Federal Transit Administration
analysis_themes: Environmental review and public assets | Project delivery and permitting
qualifiers: subject to applicable federal law [applies to: obtaining the NEPA assignment]
source_notes: The excerpt preserves the signed order's use of “National Environmental Protection Act (NEPA)” rather than silently changing the source wording.

title: BRT and bus-lane technical assistance
analysis_summary: Offer feasible planning, engineering, implementation, and cooperative-agreement assistance for BRT and bus-only infrastructure on state highways.
analysis_themes: Project delivery and permitting | Transit priority and complete trips
qualifiers: where feasible [applies to: technical assistance to local agencies for BRT, bus-only lanes, and associated infrastructure] | where possible via the cooperative agreement process [applies to: assistance with planning, engineering, and implementation]

title: Digital real-property system, holdings digitization, and condition inventory
analysis_summary: Create an implementation plan for a centralized digital real-property asset management system, fully digitize Caltrans real-estate holdings, and inventory buildings and their state of repair in coordination with DGS.
analysis_themes: Environmental review and public assets | Data, payments, and shared systems
qualifiers: In coordination with the Department of General Services [applies to: the implementation plan, holdings digitization, and building repair-state inventory]

title: Airspace policy for zero-emission fleets
analysis_summary: Identify airspace-policy changes that could allow existing operator-controlled storage sites to support zero-emission vehicle storage and energy infrastructure.
analysis_themes: Environmental review and public assets | Project delivery and permitting
qualifiers: where vehicles are already being stored [applies to: operator-controlled land beneath or adjacent to Caltrans facilities] | where consistent with state policy and safety and operational requirements [applies to: airspace exception-policy updates]

title: Excess toll-revenue guidance
analysis_summary: Develop lawful guidance for directing excess toll revenue toward unmet transit operating and capital needs, including managed-lane corridors.
analysis_themes: Funding and grants | Transit priority and complete trips
qualifiers: in accordance with state and federal law [applies to: guidance on prioritizing excess toll revenues] | where feasible [applies to: Caltrans consideration of excess revenue for transit within planned managed-lane corridors]

title: Transit updates to the California MUTCD
analysis_summary: Start a California traffic-control-manual update for transit infrastructure and seek any required federal approvals.
analysis_themes: Project delivery and permitting | Transit priority and complete trips
qualifiers: including but not limited to [applies to: transit infrastructure addressed by the MUTCD update, including transit signal priority and bus lanes] | any necessary approvals [applies to: federal approvals for the MUTCD updates]

title: Prohousing transit-funding priority
analysis_summary: Develop criteria that prioritize both funding and award processing for transit projects in Prohousing-designated jurisdictions.
collaborator_organizations: California Department of Housing and Community Development | California Transportation Commission
analysis_themes: Funding and grants | Planning and governance
qualifiers: In consultation with the Department of Housing and Community Development and CTC [applies to: development of Pro-Housing funding and award-processing criteria]

title: CHSRA NEPA delegation and programmatic review
analysis_summary: Identify and implement opportunities to use CHSRA’s FRA NEPA Delegation for State Rail Plan projects, working with the Caltrans Division of Rail and, where possible, undertaking programmatic environmental review.
mentioned_organizations: California High-Speed Rail Authority | Federal Railroad Administration
analysis_themes: Environmental review and public assets | Project delivery and permitting
qualifiers: where possible [applies to: undertaking programmatic environmental review]

title: State and federal funding support for transit priority and complete trips
analysis_summary: Update state program guidelines to incentivize transit-priority and first- and last-mile delivery, and identify federal programs that support those efforts.
lead_organizations: California State Transportation Agency | California Department of Transportation
analysis_themes: Funding and grants | Transit priority and complete trips
qualifiers: in coordination with Caltrans and the CTC [applies to: CalSTA update of state funding program guidelines] | including, but not limited to [applies to: state funding programs whose guidelines are to be updated]

title: Transit-data guidelines and grant CRM
analysis_summary: Publish transit and passenger-rail data-availability guidelines through Cal-ITP and implement a coordinated grant CRM through CIMP in partnership with Caltrans IT.
mentioned_organizations: California Integrated Travel Project | California Integrated Mobility Program | Transit grantees and subrecipients
analysis_themes: Data, payments, and shared systems | Funding and grants | Reporting and administration
qualifiers: implementing standards that allow for efficient integration between systems [applies to: guidelines for consistent transit and passenger rail data availability] | ensuring that consistent information is available to the public [applies to: transit and passenger rail data availability] | so that grantees receive streamlined, coordinated communications from a single source [applies to: implementation of the grant Customer Relationship Management system]
